REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER ON RES JUDICATA GROUNDS IS OVERRULED. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1-3 CAUSES OF ACTION AS THEY ARE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. DEMURRER TO THE 4-6 CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AS THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT BARRED BY RES JUDICATA OR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS; COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENTS ARE NOT WRITTEN. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT SILKE FINIS NOTICE OF JOINT MOTION FOR FINAL ORDER DISMISSING ACTION UNDER CCP IS DENIED. "[IF THE FIRST ACTION RESULTS IN A JUDGMENT ON THE MERITS FOR THE DEFENDANT, HIS OR HER PLEA IN ABATEMENT BECOMES A "PLEA IN BAR," I.E., INSTEAD OF A TEMPORARY OBJECTION TO TRIAL OF THE SECOND ACTION, THE DEFENDANT HAS A PERMANENT DEFENSE OF RES JUDICATA. THE DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO SET UP HIS NEW PLEA BY AMENDED OR SUPPLEMENTAL ANSWER. (SEE 5 WITKIN, CAL. PROCEDURE (5TH ED. 2008), PLEADING, 1149; LORD V. GARLAND (1946) 27 CAL.2D 840,851].) THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED OR SUPPLEMENTAL ANSWER TO ALLEGE PLEA IN BAR. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

Notice Of Joint Motion For Final Order Dismissing Action Under Ccp
Off Calendar Jun-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT SILKE FINIS Notice Of Joint Motion For Final Order Dismissing Action Under CCP is DENIED. "[If the first action results in a judgment on the merits for the defendant, his or her plea in abatement becomes a "plea in bar," i.e., instead of a temporary objection to trial of the second action, the defendant has a permanent defense of res judicata. The defendant is entitled to set up his new plea by amended or supplemental answer. (See 5 Witkin, Cal. Procedure (5th ed. 2008), Pleading, 1149; Lord v. Garland (1946) 27 Cal.2d 840,851].)

The Court grants Defendants leave to file an amended or supplemental answer to allege plea in bar. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Demmurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Cantor (415-817-1708) for Defendant/Moving party Mitchell Rosenfeld.
No other appearances.

Hearing is held. Defense counsel indicates plaintiff is contesting the ruling. Plaintiff is NOT present. Defense submits to the Tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Demurrer To Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer on res judicata grounds is OVERRULED. Moving party failed to cite any authority supporting it argument that judgment in 2010 case is "final" for the purposes of having a preclusive effect. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 1-3 causes of action as they are barred by the Statute of Limitations. Demurrer to the 4-6 causes of action is overruled as these causes of action are not barred by res judicata or statute of limitations; complaint sufficiently alleges that partnership agreements are not written.
Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
